prompt:
Create a Python file called generate_service.py that generates systemd service unit files from command-line arguments. Usage:

  python generate_service.py --name myapp --exec /usr/bin/myapp --user www-data --description 'My Application' --after network.target --restart on-failure --output myapp.service

Required args: --name, --exec
Optional args with defaults:
  --user (default: root)
  --description (default: '{name} service')
  --after (default: network.target, can specify multiple)
  --restart (default: on-failure, choices: no, on-success, on-failure, on-abnormal, on-watchdog, on-abort, always)
  --restart-sec (default: 5)
  --output (default: {name}.service)
  --wanted-by (default: multi-user.target)
  --env (repeatable, KEY=VALUE pairs to add as Environment= lines)
  --working-dir (optional WorkingDirectory=)

The generated .service file must be valid systemd unit format with [Unit], [Service], and [Install] sections.

conversation.content:
{
  "file_path": "generate_service.py",
  "content": "import argparse
import sys
import os

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Generate a systemd service unit file.")

    # Required arguments
    parser.add_argument("--name", required=True, help="Name of the service")
    parser.add_argument("--exec", required=True, help="Path to the executable")

    # Optional arguments
    parser.add_argument("--user", default="root", help="User to run the service as (default: root)")
    parser.add_argument("--description", help="Description of the service")
    parser.add_argument("--after", default="network.target", help="Units that must start before this service (default: network.target)")
    parser.add_argument("--restart",
                        choices=["no", "on-success", "on-failure", "on-abnormal", "on-watchdog", "on-abort", "always"],
                        default="on-failure",
                        help="When to restart the service (default: on-failure)")
    parser.add_argument("--restart-sec", type=int, default=5, help="Seconds to wait before restarting (default: 5)")
    parser.add_argument("--output", help="Output filename")
    parser.add_argument("--wanted-by", default="multi-user.target", help="Target for the install section (default: multi-user.target)")
    parser.add_argument("--env", action="append", help="Environment variables as KEY=VALUE pairs (repeatable)")
    parser.add_argument("--working-dir", help="Working directory for the service")

    args = parser.parse_args()

    # Handle defaults for name-dependent fields
    description = args.description if args.description else f"{args.name} service"
    output_file = args.output if args.output else f"{args.name}.service"

    # Build the service file content
    lines = ["[Unit]"]
    lines.append(f"Description={description}")
    lines.append(f"After={args.after}")
    lines.append("")

    lines.append("[Service]")
    if args.working_dir:
        lines.append(f"WorkingDirectory={args.working_dir}")
    lines.append(f"User={args.user}")
    lines.append(f"ExecStart={args.exec}")

    if args.env:
        for env_var in args.env:
            lines.append(f"Environment={env_var}")

    lines.append(f"Restart={args.restart}")
    lines.append(f"RestartSec={args.restart_sec}")
    lines.append("")

    lines.append("[Install]")
    lines.append(f"WantedBy={args.wanted_by}")

    content = "\n".join(lines) + "\n"

    try:
        with open(output_file, "w") as f:
            f.write(content)
        print(f"Successfully generated {output_file}")
    except IOError as e:
        print(f"Error writing to file {output_file}: {e}")
        sys.exit(1)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
"

files.content:
import argparse
import sys
import os

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Generate a systemd service unit file.")

    # Required arguments
    parser.add_argument("--name", required=True, help="Name of the service")
    parser.add_argument("--exec", required=True, help="Path to the executable")

    # Optional arguments
    parser.add_argument("--user", default="root", help="User to run the service as (default: root)")
    parser.add_argument("--description", help="Description of the service")
    parser.add_argument("--after", default="network.target", help="Units that must start before this service (default: network.target)")
    parser.add_argument("--restart",
                        choices=["no", "on-success", "on-failure", "on-abnormal", "on-watchdog", "on-abort", "always"],
                        default="on-failure",
                        help="When to restart the service (default: on-failure)")
    parser.add_argument("--restart-sec", type=int, default=5, help="Seconds to wait before restarting (default: 5)")
    parser.add_argument("--output", help="Output filename")
    parser.add_argument("--wanted-by", default="multi-user.target", help="Target for the install section (default: multi-user.target)")
    parser.add_argument("--env", action="append", help="Environment variables as KEY=VALUE pairs (repeatable)")
    parser.add_argument("--working-dir", help="Working directory for the service")

    args = parser.parse_args()

    # Handle defaults for name-dependent fields
    description = args.description if args.description else f"{args.name} service"
    output_file = args.output if args.output else f"{args.name}.service"

    # Build the service file content
    lines = ["[Unit]"]
    lines.append(f"Description={description}")
    lines.append(f"After={args.after}")
    lines.append("")

    lines.append("[Service]")
    if args.working_dir:
        lines.append(f"WorkingDirectory={args.working_dir}")
    lines.append(f"User={args.user}")
    lines.append(f"ExecStart={args.exec}")

    if args.env:
        for env_var in args.env:
            lines.append(f"Environment={env_var}")

    lines.append(f"Restart={args.restart}")
    lines.append(f"RestartSec={args.restart_sec}")
    lines.append("")

    lines.append("[Install]")
    lines.append(f"WantedBy={args.wanted_by}")

    content = "\n".join(lines) + "\n"

    try:
        with open(output_file, "w") as f:
            f.write(content)
        print(f"Successfully generated {output_file}")
    except IOError as e:
        print(f"Error writing to file {output_file}: {e}")
        sys.exit(1)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
